overall_assessment.one_sentence_verdict: Baseline fit is already good for AI Architect — Global/Remote because the curated record proves enterprise AI platform design, production delivery, governance-oriented integration, and architect-level modernization, but the current master-CV representation materially understates that fit.
overall_assessment.uncertainty_note: Representation proxy mode is active, and some architect-title normalization plus richer observability/greenfield scope still sits upstream rather than fully normalized in the curated metadata, so current fit is likely understated by the present headline and summary.

weighted_score_explanation: Coverage is high because curated evidence already spans enterprise AI platform architecture, RAG/retrieval quality, guardrails and access control, large-scale modernization, observability, and greenfield CQRS delivery across multiple roles.

Curation completeness is good but not complete because architect-title normalization and stronger cross-functional observability/greenfield scope still rely on vetted upstream STAR records rather than fully normalized master-CV metadata.

Representation quality is the main drag: the current headline and summary stay generic and modernization-first, while AI platform, MCP, guardrails, evaluation, and gateway evidence is pushed into projects and lower-priority role evidence.

AI architecture fit is strong because the record shows enterprise AI workflow architecture, structured outputs, tool integrations, evaluation harnesses, semantic caching, and production-oriented platform thinking rather than prototype-only experimentation.

Leadership scope fits the category's player-coach bias: mentoring, stakeholder alignment, architectural north-star setting, and workshop-style facilitation are supported, but manager/executive branding would overstate the record.

Impact proof is credible from 75% incident reduction, 3 years zero downtime, billions of daily events, 25 production tenants, 2,000 users with 42 plugins, and ~40% redundant API-spend reduction in testing, although AI-specific outcome metrics are not yet concentrated in top CV real estate.

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Enterprise AI platform architecture with real enterprise scope
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: This category rewards architects who can show target-state AI platform design tied to production usage, integrations, and operational boundaries.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by Commander-4/Joyia evidence showing platform lead scope for an enterprise AI workflow platform serving 2,000 users with 42 plugins plus structured outputs, 5 MCP tools, per-silo guardrails, and access control.

strongest_supported_signals.signal: RAG, retrieval quality, and evaluation architecture
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: RAG and evaluation are differentiators in this market when they are framed as architecture components rather than isolated experiments.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by hybrid BM25+RRF retrieval, Claude/LiteLLM reranking, MRR@k/NDCG@k evaluation functions, and a retrieval evaluation harness validated with 14 unit tests.

strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Secure enterprise rollout depends on controls around outputs, tool integrations, and tenancy boundaries, not just model calls.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by Zod schema validation for all LLM responses, 5 MCP server tools, per-silo guardrail injection via LiteLLM proxy, and per-silo access control.

strongest_supported_signals.signal: Large-scale modernization and distributed-systems architecture foundation
strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: Non-AI architecture proof strengthens AI architect credibility when it demonstrates modernization, service-boundary design, resilience, and production rigor.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by transformation of a fragile AdTech platform into event-driven TypeScript microservices on AWS with 75% incident reduction, 3 years zero downtime, and DDD adoption that improved onboarding and velocity.

strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: The category values architects who can handle both modernization and greenfield delivery, especially when the latter shows architecture choices under business pressure.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by architecting and launching a 0→1 event-sourced CRM platform with 25 production tenants, leading event storming with stakeholders, and making scalability decisions for 10x growth.

strongest_supported_signals.why_it_matters_for_category: This market favors senior IC architects who align stakeholders and coach engineers without depending on formal people-management scope.
strongest_supported_signals.citation: Supported by establishing an architectural north star, educating stakeholders on technical-debt ROI, mentoring 10+ engineers with 3 promotions to lead, and translating business metrics into technical realization.

gap_analysis.signal: AI architect branding in headline and metadata
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: This category strongly prefers architect-family branding, and the current top-line identity still reads more generic-leadership than AI-architect.
gap_analysis.current_state: The current headline is 'Engineering Leader / Software Architect', while the strongest curated evidence is enterprise AI platform architecture, gateway/integration controls, and production AI delivery.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: The record safely supports architect-first AI branding, but not executive AI branding.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Surface an architect-family AI headline and metadata variant anchored in enterprise AI platforms, integration, evaluation, and guardrails rather than generic engineering leadership.
gap_analysis.citation: Underrepresented in [role_metadata.json:3] relative to curated AI architecture proof in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332], and [projects/lantern.md:7].

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: The summary is the fastest place to prove category fit; if it leads only with AdTech modernization, AI architect fit will read as secondary.
gap_analysis.current_state: The current summary is dominated by legacy modernization on AWS and does not foreground Commander-4, MCP integrations, guardrails, evaluation, or LLM gateway work.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: The record supports a summary that leads with enterprise AI platform design and then uses modernization as enabling foundation.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Rewrite the summary so AI platform/gateway/governance evidence is primary and legacy modernization is framed as the production architecture substrate.
gap_analysis.citation: The current summary in [role_metadata.json:3] omits stronger AI-specific evidence already curated in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332], [projects/commander4.md:8], and [projects/lantern.md:7].

gap_analysis.signal: Current-role architect title alias and scope normalization
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Architect title-family alignment would materially improve category fit, but title normalization must stay provenance-safe.
gap_analysis.current_state: Curated metadata uses 'Technical Lead (Addressable TV)', while vetted upstream STAR records repeatedly describe the scope as 'Lead Software Engineer / Technical Architect'.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Do not overwrite the canonical role title without provenance policy, but a normalized architect-scope alias or metadata note appears supportable.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Promote the architect-title alias and architect-scope wording into role metadata with explicit provenance, while preserving the canonical employment title.
gap_analysis.citation: Not yet curated from upstream title evidence in [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:7], [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:98], and [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:181].

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: This category values architects who can modernize existing estates and build new platforms; the current representation leans too heavily toward modernization only.
gap_analysis.current_state: Top current-role representation emphasizes AdTech modernization, while greenfield CRM architecture, event storming, and tenant-launch evidence remain secondary.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: The record safely supports both modernization and greenfield architect positioning.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Bring one Samdock bullet into the core narrative to balance Seven.One modernization with 0→1 architecture ownership.
gap_analysis.citation: Greenfield evidence is curated in [02_samdock_daypaio.md:27], [02_samdock_daypaio.md:44], and [02_samdock_daypaio.md:142] but is not reflected in the current top-level representation anchored by [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28].

gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Global/remote AI architect roles often reward architects who can run workshops, translate requirements, and align client or enterprise stakeholders.
gap_analysis.current_state: Stakeholder collaboration exists across Seven.One, Samdock, and Clary Icon, but it is not intentionally surfaced as architect-style workshop/translation proof.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: The record supports stakeholder translation, event-storming facilitation, and direct client collaboration; it does not automatically support a default customer-facing solution architect identity.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Surface stakeholder workshops, event storming, and direct client communication as secondary architect signals tied to delivery outcomes.
gap_analysis.citation: Supported and curated in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:250], [02_samdock_daypaio.md:44], and [06_clary_icon.md:103], but underrepresented in the current representation.

gap_analysis.signal: Explicit agent-workflow ownership beyond tool-calling and workflow plugins
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: The category rewards agent/orchestration proof, but overstating from adjacent evidence would create credibility risk.
gap_analysis.current_state: Curated evidence shows workflow plugins, MCP tools, guardrail injection, structured outputs, and enterprise AI workflow delivery, but does not explicitly prove shipped autonomous agent workflows or broad agentic ownership.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Safe wording is MCP tools, workflow plugins, tool-calling architecture, and enterprise AI workflows; broad 'agentic AI leader' wording is too strong.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Keep agent-language subordinate until explicit shipped agent workflows, routing logic, or agent-operability outcomes are curated with provenance.
gap_analysis.citation: Current evidence supports integration and workflow architecture in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332] and [projects/commander4.md:9], but not a stronger autonomous-agent claim.

gap_analysis.signal: AI-specific outcome metrics concentrated in top bullets
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: Architect profiles become more competitive when AI architecture claims are paired with visible quality, latency, cost, and scale outcomes.
gap_analysis.current_state: The curated record contains AI metrics and outcome fragments, but the top representation does not consolidate them into flagship bullets.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Safe metrics include 2,000 users, 42 plugins, 14 tests, ~2ms L1 cache, ~200ms L2 cache, and ~40% redundant API-spend reduction in testing.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Promote AI-specific scale/quality/cost metrics into top role/project bullets rather than leaving them dispersed across project lines and lower-level evidence.
gap_analysis.citation: Metrics exist in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:354], [projects/commander4.md:8], and [projects/lantern.md:8], but are not concentrated in current top-level representation.

gap_analysis.signal: AI compliance ownership and region-specific regulation authority
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: This category values governance, but unsafe compliance inflation would weaken credibility for global remote architect roles.
gap_analysis.current_state: Curated evidence proves GDPR/TCF delivery in AdTech and guardrail/access-control design in AI systems, but not ownership of AI compliance programs, EU AI Act implementation, or broad regulatory authority for AI.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Safe wording is guardrails, access control, structured outputs, and prior compliance delivery experience in non-AI systems.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Avoid claiming AI compliance ownership; keep governance language tied to technical controls already evidenced.
gap_analysis.citation: [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:106] supports GDPR/TCF delivery and [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332] supports AI guardrails, but neither safely supports AI compliance-owner positioning.

gap_analysis.signal: Provider-branded or multi-cloud AI architect positioning
gap_analysis.why_it_matters: The blueprint explicitly warns against default cloud-authority branding when provider evidence is supporting rather than category-defining.
gap_analysis.current_state: AWS delivery is strong in modernization work, and Lantern shows multi-provider routing across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Azure endpoints, but this is not the same as broad provider-authority or multi-cloud architect branding.
gap_analysis.safe_interpretation: Safe wording is AWS-backed modernization plus multi-provider LLM gateway design; unsafe wording is defaulting to AWS/Azure or multi-cloud AI architect authority.
gap_analysis.recommended_action: Keep provider names as supporting bullets rather than headline anchors unless future curation adds broader platform scope.
gap_analysis.citation: Supported provider-adjacent evidence exists in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28] and [projects/lantern.md:7], but broader authority claims would still overreach.

headline_safe: AI Architect | Enterprise AI Platforms, RAG, Evaluation & Guardrails
AI Solution Architect | Enterprise Integration, MCP Tools, Workflow Platforms
Software Architect | AI Platforms, Modernization, Distributed Systems
profile_safe: Senior architect-first IC who designs enterprise AI platforms and carries target architectures into production systems.
Hands-on architect with evidence in hybrid retrieval, evaluation harnesses, semantic caching, structured outputs, model routing, and governance controls.
Enterprise modernization architect whose distributed-systems foundation strengthens GenAI delivery credibility.
experience_safe: Platform lead for an enterprise AI workflow platform serving 2,000 users with 42 plugins.
Designed structured-output and tool-calling architecture with 5 MCP tools, per-silo guardrails, and access control.
Built hybrid retrieval, LLM-as-judge reranking, semantic caching, and retrieval evaluation for enterprise AI search quality.
Transformed a legacy AdTech platform into event-driven AWS microservices with 75% incident reduction and 3 years zero downtime.
Architected and launched a 0→1 event-sourced CRM platform that went live with 25 production tenants.
leadership_safe: Established architectural north-star direction and educated stakeholders on technical-debt ROI.
Mentored 10+ engineers on architecture and cloud patterns, with 3 promoted to lead roles.
Facilitated stakeholder workshops and domain discovery through event storming and business-metric translation.
unsafe_or_too_weak: Head of AI, Director of AI, VP AI, or other executive AI ownership titles.
Agentic AI leader phrasing that implies shipped autonomous-agent programs rather than evidenced workflow/tool integrations.
AI compliance owner, EU AI Act authority, or responsible-AI program ownership.
Multi-cloud AI architect authority as a default brand.
Formal people-manager or org-builder identity as the primary category frame.
Research-heavy, fine-tuning, RLHF, or advanced-experimentation positioning.

well_represented_now: The current representation already shows strong legacy modernization and distributed-systems architecture proof from Seven.One.
Commander-4 and Lantern projects visibly surface RAG, gateway, caching, and evaluation depth.
The skill stack already contains many of the right AI-platform keywords, including RAG Pipeline, Retrieval Evaluation, MCP Tool Design, LLM Gateway Design, and Model Routing.
underrepresented_now: AI architect branding is weaker than the underlying evidence and is not the default top-line identity.
Guardrails, structured outputs, MCP integrations, and access control are not central in the current summary despite being high-value category signals.
Greenfield architecture and workshop-style stakeholder facilitation are present in curated evidence but not part of the current top narrative.
Player-coach leadership is supported, but the present 'Engineering Leader' phrasing risks reading manager-first instead of architect-first.
overstated_risk_now: The headline 'Engineering Leader / Software Architect' can be read as broader management scope than the evidence needs for this category.
The current summary overweights AdTech modernization and underweights current AI platform architecture, which can make the record look adjacent rather than directly targeted.
The skills list is tool-dense; without stronger architecture-first framing, it risks reading as implementation inventory instead of enterprise AI architecture proof.
representation_priorities: Make the top line architect-first and AI-explicit without inflating to executive or provider-authority branding.
Move Commander-4 governance/integration/evaluation evidence into summary and current-role priority bullets.
Use one Samdock greenfield bullet to balance Seven.One modernization and strengthen the architect narrative across lifecycle modes.
Keep leadership language in player-coach terms: mentoring, stakeholder alignment, architecture direction, and workshop facilitation.

curation_priorities.action: Promote the vetted 'Technical Architect' alias and architect-scope description for the current Seven.One role into normalized metadata with provenance preserved.
curation_priorities.why_now: Architect-family title alignment is the single highest-leverage curation gap for this category because it improves top-line fit without fabricating new scope.
curation_priorities.expected_category_impact: Unlocks safer AI Architect / AI Solution Architect positioning and reduces dependence on the generic 'Engineering Leader' label.
curation_priorities.citation: Upstream architect-title support is present in [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:7], [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:98], and [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:181].

curation_priorities.action: Promote STAR-record observability-platform details for Seven.One into curated role evidence, especially cross-functional architecture ownership, sub-second latency, and 10x cost reduction.
curation_priorities.why_now: This category rewards production architecture, scale, and operationalization; the upstream STAR record adds stronger quantified proof than the current top bullet selection.
curation_priorities.expected_category_impact: Strengthens enterprise platform design, operational rigor, and scale/reliability proof for architect screening.
curation_priorities.citation: High-signal upstream observability architecture evidence appears in [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:268].

curation_priorities.action: Promote the Daypaio Tech Lead STAR framing into normalized Samdock metadata and role evidence, emphasizing end-to-end architecture, event sourcing, and startup-to-enterprise tradeoffs.
curation_priorities.why_now: Greenfield architecture is a category differentiator and currently sits below its true strategic value in the master-CV representation.
curation_priorities.expected_category_impact: Improves the greenfield-plus-modernization balance and reinforces senior IC architect scope beyond the current employer.
curation_priorities.citation: Additional greenfield architect scope is available upstream in [docs/archive/knowledge-base.md:734].

master_cv_upgrade_actions.action: Replace the generic leadership-first top line with an architect-first AI title family that reflects enterprise AI platform design rather than people-management breadth.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.why_now: Headline fit is currently the largest representation drag for this category.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.citation: Current headline in [role_metadata.json:3] underrepresents AI-architect proof already curated in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332], and [projects/lantern.md:7].

master_cv_upgrade_actions.action: Rewrite the summary so Commander-4, MCP/guardrails, evaluation, and gateway architecture lead, with legacy modernization reframed as the production-delivery foundation.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.why_now: The current summary makes the profile look modernization-first instead of AI-architect-first.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.citation: The summary in [role_metadata.json:3] should be realigned to the stronger AI evidence in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332], and [projects/lantern.md:7].

master_cv_upgrade_actions.action: Reorder current-role priority bullets so AI platform architecture, observability/scale, and legacy modernization sit together as the flagship architect story.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.why_now: The strongest category fit comes from the combination of AI platform delivery plus proven enterprise architecture under production constraints.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.citation: The current role already contains the needed flagship evidence in [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:71], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], and [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332].

master_cv_upgrade_actions.action: Keep Commander-4 and Lantern as proof projects, but rewrite their emphasis toward architecture outcomes, governance, evaluation, and enterprise integration rather than tool density alone.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.why_now: The projects already carry highly relevant AI-architect proof; they need tighter category framing, not more stack detail.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.citation: Project evidence in [projects/commander4.md:8], [projects/commander4.md:9], [projects/lantern.md:7], and [projects/lantern.md:10] supports stronger architect-oriented project framing.

master_cv_upgrade_actions.action: Rebalance the skills section toward solution architecture, enterprise AI platform design, integration, guardrails, evaluation, model routing, and distributed-systems delivery; demote generic tool inventory.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.why_now: The category rewards architecture and delivery outcomes more than exhaustive framework enumeration.
master_cv_upgrade_actions.citation: Curated evidence already supports a higher-level skills hierarchy through [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:28], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:282], [01_seven_one_entertainment.md:332], and [02_samdock_daypaio.md:27].

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Lead with architect-family AI branding, not generic engineering-leadership branding.
Center the category story on enterprise AI platform design, governance controls, and production delivery.
Use legacy modernization and distributed-systems architecture as foundational proof for AI architect credibility.
Balance modernization evidence with a greenfield architecture story from Samdock.
Promote the upstream 'Technical Architect' alias into normalized metadata with provenance-safe handling.
Keep leadership framing in player-coach terms: mentoring, architectural direction, and stakeholder alignment.
Do not claim broad agentic-AI ownership beyond workflow, tool-calling, and MCP integration evidence.
Do not claim AI compliance-owner or multi-cloud AI-architect authority from adjacent evidence.
Concentrate AI-specific scale, latency, quality, and cost metrics in flagship bullets.
